title: Frontend: dependency-free service status page
prompt: Build a service status dashboard as a static page (index.html plus vanilla ES modules, no npm dependencies and no build step). Render a list of services from a JSON data module with name, status (operational | degraded | down), and lastChecked. Show a summary banner that is green only when every service is operational, support filtering by status, and sort outages first. Put the summary/filter/sort logic in a pure ES module separate from DOM code and cover it with node --test unit tests, including the empty-services and all-down edge cases.
judgeFocus: pure logic module separated from DOM code and unit tested
banner state correct for mixed, all-operational, and all-down data
no dependencies or build step introduced

scale.1: Fails the dimension or has no usable evidence.
scale.2: Has major gaps, regressions, or mostly claim-based evidence.
scale.3: Acceptable but incomplete, noisy, or only partially evidenced.
scale.4: Strong result with minor gaps or limited missing evidence.
scale.5: Excellent result with complete behavior and strong evidence.

criticalRequirements: The page must render service states, filter by status, and sort outages first.
The summary banner must be green only when every service is operational.
Summary/filter/sort logic must live in a pure module covered by node --test tests.
The fixture baseline test must remain green with no npm dependencies added.

dimensions.description: How completely the final workspace implements rendering, the summary banner, filtering, and outage-first sorting described in the prompt.
Whether tests pass and the logic handles mixed, empty, and all-down service data without regressions.
Maintainability, real separation between pure logic and DOM wiring, small focused diff, and meaningful unit coverage.
Quality of planning artifacts, contract specificity, and repair-loop behavior relative to the ceremony level used.
Whether verdicts are backed by reproducible command evidence rather than claims.

scoringNotes: Do not change this rubric based on run-generated eval criteria or contracts.
Logic embedded directly in DOM event handlers without a testable pure module should cap implementationQuality at 3.
Prefer concrete test output evidence over implementation claims.

notes: Part of the ceremony-ladder benchmark suite. Designed to compare ceremony levels and providers on identical frontend work.
